text: If the You-tuber Tom Stanton can make a flywheels Battery how givs light up 20 Led! This Watergenerator should give at least 2 volt or more and can bee a reservesystem for the light and radio in our houses! The water is driven in by high levels in our communitys a reserve we all have! As long Water is function a reserve system is in the house! A great idea I think! Thanks Rob!🙂

author: @dans-designs
text: I love resin printing! Ive been working on a small scale axial flux generator to provide a robot around 12v for continuous operation, the generator is powered by a small 3v drone motor (similar to the one you showed in a previous video) connected to a solar panel for day-time operation and a small AA battery for night-time. Im working on getting a supercapacitor in the mix, charged by the output of the generator itself.. Id love to get your thoughts on this!

author: @kgsalvage6306
text: I made a magnetic bearing exactly like that a couple years ago for a pulse motor I built.  It worked great.  Would spin forever. I used a stainless steel shaft and a bronze bearing with some of this super slick lubricant I use.

author: @TheBaconWizard
text:
Interesting thing about those printers: The bottom screen is made out of FEP,  which is very similar indeed to PTFE both in terms of triboelectric performance and as a dielectric. But it will melt at around 271degC. The screens tend to be quite expensive but with a bit of effort it can be found in anything from 25microns upwards in larger quantities and suitably reduced price (Hong-Kong Uni's version uses dense PTFE at 16microns) 
I have some coming from Aliexpress for the purpose of making a tribo-generator with your ink among other ideas. 
I'm thinking I can use the ink in the microwave to heat a sheet of FEP and get it to bond to the ink/aluminium back-plate and give us a water-proof cell with hopefully some good results. Plenty of ways to screw this up though, and I usually manage to find them ALL before achieving the goal, lol. 
Sorry to go off-topic here! I think we quite enjoy watching you get soaked XD

author: @johnknight8152
text: You could convert a gutter system with a bunged end and a nozzle for the horizontal jet, the back pressure depending on the drop may be quite powerful I assume.

author: @manekou3303
text: Is it possible to rearrange the setup so the bearing and coils are seperated from the pelton wheel by a sheild of some kind so you dont have to worry that much about wet electronics? (Also could you add more coils to the generator to see how much energy can be drawn from the wheel before you hit the maximum?) Also is it possible to make coils from conductive ink?

author: @giannagiavelli5098
text: You need a design for a vertical magnetic bearing so it can be used with a pond stream divertor etc. And of course many more coils!

replies.author: @Marian_Martin_Morgenstern
replies.text:
put a third Magnet on top of the floating one with opposing poles and attach it to the axle. Turn it vertical and design something to stick it in.
But to be honest, horizontal is just as fine for a stream divertor, just turn the outlet 90°

author: @michaelmeenaghan8559
text: Would it not be a better description saying magnetically levitated, as the bearing is not magnetic. Unless you like water all over the place, could not compressed air serve for demonstration purposes, proof of concept.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think pseudo magnetic levitation is the more frequently used term mate - and can you imagine the comments I would get if I used air instead of water?

replies.author: @michaelmeenaghan8559
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Thank you for the reply. Having watched again it appears there is no bearing, had this been mounted horizontally then no need for the supporting magnets. Air/water, as a driving force, I imagine not many would have a problem with the demo.

author: @David_Mash

Loved this video! Thank You! 
It's funny because the pelton spoon is designed to direct the water right back at the source so I imagine the better your results, the more wet you got haha
